prompts:
I need to design an iOS and iPad application that allows us to use agentics and chat directly with claude/codex/pi agent instances running on a VPS. Most of these cli tools have an integrated WS server and I want to leverage or redesign on specifically for this new app.

I want to create an interface that uses LibGhostty and this repo as a "starting point" https://github.com/holzschu/a-Shell

But we will quickly innovate away from this design. First:
The users will not need to chat directly in ghostty terminal interface. We will fake this experience and use a high-powered agentic keyboard that allows for various basic features supported in a-shell, but adds an innovative "quick action" tool. This tool will load a different "snippet" focused keyboard containing stored prompt templates (push to git, explain what you did, open worktree, etc)

The terminal interface itself will be considered a visual canvas of agentic activity. We will parse event data and logs from clis on VPS and display event data in actionable and meaningful way. For example:
- Pretty git diff
- Reasoning
- Skill/command runs
- Sub agent ticker and view

For sub agents, design this app with three pages "home" "work" "Ais"

Home can remain simple for now. default page is work, this is the terminal page. Ai's page is a "DM" type of interface to chat directly with Ai agents. This view should be a "mini" work view and allow users to check directly on Ai agents doing work. In order to accomplish, we will need to use TMUX (on vps or local in app?) so we can attach to sessions.

We should be able to promote agent sessions to main "work" view

Focus on thinking through this problem with immense care and consideration. You can integrate and use this MCP for looking up docs: https://github.com/kimsungwhee/apple-docs-mcp

You can review the skills here related to MacOs development:
https://github.com/Dimillian/Skills
